title: Tithymalus × peisonis
intro: Tithymalus × peisonis, also known as the Peisander milk-thistle, is a hybrid plant species within the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its thistle-like appearance. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective properties, which can help protect the liver from damage and improve overall liver function. The bioactive constituents of Tithymalus × peisonis include flavonoids, such as silymarin, and other polyphenolic compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, including capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to support liver health and to treat conditions such as jaundice, cirrhosis, and fatty liver disease.
taxonomy: Tithymalus × peisonis is a hybrid plant that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a cross between two species within the Tithymalus genus. The classification of this plant suggests that it exhibits characteristics from both parent species. Tithymalus × peisonis is often classified as a subspecies or variety of one of its parent species. Further research may be needed to determine the exact classification of this hybrid plant.
